title: Full-stack scaffold: tiny notes API + static client + integration test
strength: Coordinated server + client + test scaffold where the layers must agree on a contract.
prompt: Scaffold a tiny full-stack notes app in this empty Node project. Backend: server.js exporting an Express `app` (do not auto-listen when required) with GET /api/notes (returns all notes as JSON array) and POST /api/notes (accepts {text}, validates non-empty, assigns an incrementing id, returns 201 with the created note; returns 400 {error} on empty text). Notes are stored in memory. Frontend: public/index.html plus public/app.js that fetches /api/notes on load, renders them into a <ul id="notes">, and has a form (#note-form with input #note-text) that POSTs a new note and re-renders. Keep it dependency-light vanilla JS, no framework. Add a bin/serve.js that imports app and listens on process.env.PORT||3000. Write an integration test test/api.test.js using supertest that covers: empty list initially, POST creates a note returning 201 + id, the note then appears in GET, and POST with empty text returns 400. Wire package.json `test` script to jest. All tests must pass.
verify: node -e "const a=require('./server'); if(typeof a!=='function' && typeof a.listen!=='function'){process.exit(1)}"
